data.title: Suspended US law professor and Israel critic sues to block campus probe
data.description:
By Karen Sloan

A University of Kentucky law professor sued the university on Thursday, claiming it violated his free speech and due process rights this summer when it removed him from teaching, banned him from campus, and launched an investigation into his criticisms of Israel at academic conferences and in online forums.

Ramsi Woodcock said in the lawsuit, filed in U.S. District Court for the Eastern District of Kentucky, that the probe was baseless and was spurred by political pressure from state lawmakers to crack down on antisemitism on college campuses.

The complaint said the inquiry has chilled speech at the Lexington, Kentucky university, and asked the court to rule that a recently-adopted Kentucky state law aimed at combating campus antisemitism violates the U.S. Constitution.

“Faculty are afraid to protest Professor Woodcock’s suspension, students are afraid to speak about Palestine, and some are considering separating from the university as a result,” the lawsuit said.

University spokesperson Jay Blanton said Friday that Woodcock has not been suspended but has been "reassigned" during the investigation, which began in July. The right of faculty members to free expression “doesn’t extend to creating a hostile environment for people,” Blanton said.

Woodcock, who is of Arab descent, also alleged racial discrimination in the lawsuit. He did not immediately respond to a request for comment on Friday.

He is at least the third U.S. law professor since January to be barred from teaching or fired in response to politically charged speech on issues such as Israel's war with Hamas in Gaza and the murder of conservative activist Charlie Kirk. Debates over campus free speech have intensified during President Donald Trump's second term, with his administration cracking down on protests in what it has positioned as an effort to combat antisemitism.

Woodcock said in his complaint that he began speaking publicly against “Israeli colonialism” in early 2024, including by circulating a “Petition for Military Action Against Israel” online and sharing his views on law faculty listservs. He said he never spoke about his views on Israel in the classroom.

The university informed that he was under investigation in July, after state lawmakers passed the campus antisemitism law in April, the lawsuit said.

Read more:

University of Arkansas fires law professor over Charlie Kirk comments

Judge tosses law professor Amy Wax's bias lawsuit over UPenn sanctions

Free speech is a hot topic at law schools. Should the ABA get involved?

data.title: SPIRE INC SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Spire Inc., a holding company for several gas utilities and gas-related businesses, has released its annual Form 10-K report, detailing its financial and operational performance for the fiscal year. The report provides insights into the company's revenue, income, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces in the current market environment.

Financial Highlights

Spire Inc. reported the following key financial metrics for the fiscal year:

  • Operating Revenues: $2,476.4 million, reflecting a decrease from the previous year's $2,593.0 million, primarily due to lower gas cost recoveries.
  • Operating Income: $523.9 million, an increase from $488.3 million in the prior year, driven by higher contribution margins and cost management.
  • Net Income: $271.7 million, up from $250.9 million in the previous year, supported by improved operating income and lower interest expenses.
  • Diluted Earnings Per Share: $4.37, compared to $4.19 in the prior year, reflecting the increase in net income and efficient share management.

Business Highlights

Spire Inc. operates in three segments: Gas Utility, Gas Marketing, and Midstream, focusing on organic growth, infrastructure investment, and innovation. Key operational highlights include:

  • Gas Utility Segment: This segment includes regulated operations of Spire Missouri, Spire Alabama, Spire Gulf, and Spire Mississippi. The business is seasonal, with earnings concentrated during the heating season from November to April.
  • Gas Marketing Segment: Spire Marketing provides natural gas marketing services, leveraging market expertise and risk management to optimize its portfolio. The segment serves a diverse customer base, primarily in the central and southern U.S.
  • Midstream Segment: Includes Spire STL Pipeline, Spire MoGas Pipeline, and Spire Storage operations. The segment focuses on transportation and storage of natural gas, supporting grid reliability and managing gas price volatility.
  • Human Capital Resources: As of September 30, 2025, Spire employed 3,497 people. The company emphasizes safety, employee development, and inclusion, with programs to reduce workplace hazards and promote well-being.
  • Safety Initiatives: Spire has implemented safety programs like the Good Catch program and Field Safety Observations to proactively identify and mitigate workplace hazards, resulting in improved driving metrics in 2025.
  • Employee Development: Spire offers comprehensive development resources, including training programs and tuition assistance, to enhance leadership and technical skills across all organizational levels.
  • Gas Utility Operations: Spire Missouri and Spire Alabama are the largest natural gas distribution utilities in their respective states, serving over 1.6 million customers combined. They focus on reliable service and infrastructure improvements.
  • Natural Gas Supply Strategy: The Utilities aim to ensure dependable and economically priced gas supply, utilizing diverse sources and strategic transportation arrangements to mitigate price volatility.
  • Storage Capacity: Spire Missouri and Spire Alabama have significant storage capacities to meet peak demand, with Spire Missouri having rights to store 22.0 Bcf of gas in MRT’s storage facility.
  • Regulatory Environment: The Utilities operate under various regulatory frameworks, with mechanisms like the Purchased Gas Adjustment (PGA) and Gas Supply Adjustment (GSA) to manage gas cost recovery.
  • Future Outlook: Spire plans to expand its regulated utility footprint through the acquisition of Piedmont Natural Gas's Tennessee business, expected to close in early 2026, enhancing growth and shareholder value.

Strategic Initiatives

Spire Inc. has outlined several strategic initiatives and capital management activities:

  • Acquisition of Piedmont Natural Gas's Tennessee Business: Spire entered into an agreement to acquire Piedmont Natural Gas's Tennessee business for $2.48 billion, aiming to expand its regulated utility footprint and increase its scale in high-quality jurisdictions. The acquisition is expected to close in the first quarter of 2026, subject to regulatory approvals. Spire is considering selling its natural gas storage facilities to help fund the acquisition.
  • Capital Management: Spire issued $200 million in First Mortgage Bonds to support general corporate purposes. The company also increased its debt by $478 million in fiscal 2025, while reducing long-term debt by $335 million. Spire's equity distribution agreement allows for the sale of additional shares up to $123.6 million through January 2027, though activity under this program is currently suspended. The company declared dividends totaling $51.1 million as of September 30, 2025.
  • Future Outlook: Spire plans to finance the Piedmont acquisition through a balanced mix of debt, equity, and hybrid securities. The company anticipates capital expenditures of $809 million for fiscal 2026, focusing on infrastructure upgrades and new business opportunities. Spire aims to maintain a strong balance sheet and ensure sufficient liquidity to meet future requirements, supported by its investment-grade credit ratings.

Challenges and Risks

Spire Inc. faces several challenges and risks, including:

  • Regulatory and Legislative Risks: The company faces significant regulatory and legislative risks, particularly related to climate change initiatives that could increase compliance costs and impact demand for natural gas. The potential enactment of new regulations or changes in existing laws could adversely affect operations and financial results. The SEC's climate disclosure rules, although currently stayed, could require significant expenditures if implemented.
  • Operational Risks: The Utilities are subject to regulation by federal, state, and local authorities, which impacts rates, costs, and profitability. There is a risk of disallowance of cost recovery, which could affect financial results. The expiration of the ISRS statute in 2029 poses a risk to revenue timing and cash flows. Operational risks include the potential for significant costs and liabilities due to federal safety and integrity regulations related to pipeline and storage operations. The rescission of the PHMSA advisory bulletin adds uncertainty to future regulatory requirements.
  • Environmental and Legal Risks: Environmental laws and regulations may require significant expenditures, and the overturning of the Chevron Doctrine adds uncertainty to regulatory interpretations. The Utilities' liquidity may be affected by delays in cost recovery due to regulation. The company is exposed to risks from changes in income tax policy, which could impact the value of net operating losses and financial results. Legal proceedings could also adversely affect financial condition if outcomes require payments beyond provisions.
  • Market and Competitive Risks: Commodity markets and derivative instruments are regulated, and new developments could impact Spire Marketing's operations. The company faces risks from market competition, which could affect customer retention and acquisition.
  • Acquisition Risks: The acquisition of Piedmont Natural Gas's Tennessee business presents risks, including potential delays in regulatory approvals and integration challenges. The transaction is expected to close in early 2026, subject to conditions.
  • Market Risks: The company is exposed to market risks from natural gas price fluctuations, which could impact liquidity and financial results. The use of derivative instruments to hedge risks may not fully protect against volatility.
  • Operational Disruptions: Operational risks include the potential for disruptions in natural gas supply and transportation, which could affect financial performance. The company is also vulnerable to cyberattacks, which could disrupt operations and lead to financial losses.
  • Economic and Accounting Risks: The company's financial condition could be impacted by economic downturns, which may reduce energy consumption and affect customer payments. Changes in accounting standards could also affect financial results.

SEC Filing: SPIRE INC [ SR ] - 10-K - Nov. 14, 2025

data.title: Applied Materials' F4Q Results Seen as 'Uninspiring' — Market Talk
data.description: Applied Materials' F4Q results didn't provide the positive catalyst that Morgan Stanley analysts expected, they say. "Numbers and comments were uninspiring," according to the analysts, who also note that concerns remain over Applied's China business and its internet of things, communications, automotive, power and sensors segment. "There was very little in the results that could alter the narrative, and we came away more disappointed than encouraged," they say. The analysts do not think the stock will outperform the equipment industry in the near-term, unless there are material changes in the operating environment, they say, lowering their price target on the stock to $252 from $256. (kelly.cloonan@wsj.com)

data.title: Private oil investment in Colombia expected to fall in 2026, industry group says
data.description:
By Nelson Bocanegra

Private oil and gas investment in Colombia is expected to fall again in 2026, but companies could reverse course late in the year after presidential elections, the head of the country’s main industry group said on Friday.

Leftist President Gustavo Petro - whose term ends in August next year - halted new hydrocarbon exploration contracts as part of his push to reduce Colombia’s dependence on fossil fuels and promote solar and wind energy projects, efforts that have met with mixed success.

Oil output and investment both dropped year-on-year in 2024 and are expected to do the same in 2025.

Oil companies' spending will focus mainly on obligations under existing contracts in 2026, with reduced exploration, said Colombian Petroleum Association (ACP) President Frank Pearl.

"With some exceptions, but in general we believe investment will decline next year," he said.

Pearl had previously forecast an 8% rise in investment in hydrocarbon exploration and production this year, but the projection will not be met, he said.

"There is no scenario where we can see a recovery," Pearl told Reuters, adding exploratory activity in the South American country has dropped more than 60% over the past three years.

Colombia was producing an average of 750,000 barrels per day of oil in August.

Pearl said he expects the oil industry to rebound based on proposals from most presidential hopefuls.

"Next year could be split in two — not in terms of investment flows because they won't arrive in time, but in terms of intention and commitment," he said. "If a new administration sends clear signals that the sector will be revitalized, some investors will start preparing to invest in suspended contracts or seek new areas and secure new deals."

Still, any recovery will be slow given industry timelines.

"From the moment a project starts until a molecule reaches the market can easily take six years," Pearl said, adding that Colombia has gone four years without a single new contract.

"Now that will probably happen only by 2032 if everything goes well."

data.title: PLATTS: 1107--Platts Americas Wind and Solar Renewable Capture Price Indexes Daily Commentary - 14Nov25
data.description: (Platts)--14Nov2025/229 pm EST/1929 GMT **ERCOT increased just under $20/MWh on average **ISONE capture rates averaged above $80/MWh Renewable capture prices traded mostly up across the US on Nov. 12, with the Electric Reliability Council of Texas and New England's Independent System Operator observing the two most consistent increases. ERCOT ERCOT saw solar capture rates across its three locations rise about $22.75 on average to around $23.50/MWh. Wind trended similarly, with the North and West locations both rising more than $20 to an average of $23.50/MWh. However, the South wind hub saw the only decrease on the day, falling $1.54 to minus $6.85/MWh. ERCOT's West location saw the greatest wind and solar capture price increases, rising around $27.50 and $24.50, respectively, to $21.87/MWh and $23.39/MWh. The majority of capture prices surged with regional temperature averages, rising 6.7 F to 64.3 F (17.9 C) on Nov. 12, according to CustomWeather. Wind's total supply within the same period decreased 200.74 GWh to 336.03 GWh, according to US Energy Information Administration data. ISONE, SPP New England ISO solar capture prices climbed $24.26 to 89.06/MWh, while wind rates increased $10.36 to $74.61/MWh. Climbing capture prices also tracked rising average temperatures, which increased 3.9 F to 41.1 F, according to CustomWeather. The Southwest Power Pool saw similar increases for its two wind hubs, rising $15.38 at the North hub to $27.49/MWh and rising $9.55 at the South hub to $28.03/MWh. CustomWeather's SPP average temperatures increased 4.9 F to 53.1 F. Platts is part of S&P Global Commodity Insights. — Ricardo Plata iii, ricardo.plata.iii@spglobal.com-- Edited by Jim Levesque, newsdesk@spglobal.com--Platts Electricity Alert--

data.title: FAST CASUAL CONCEPTS, INC. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Fast Casual Concepts, Inc., a company that has recently transitioned from franchising casual dining establishments to focusing on digital marketing services, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter of 2025. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance and significant operational changes during the nine months ended September 30, 2025.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenues: $44,600,000 for the nine months ended September 30, 2025, reflecting the company's entry into digital marketing services.
  • Operating Loss: $(4,620,000) for the nine months ended September 30, 2025, indicating a reduction in losses compared to the previous year.
  • Net Income (Loss) from Continuing Operations: $(14,061,000) for the nine months ended September 30, 2025, showing improvement from the prior year's loss.
  • Net Income (Loss) from Discontinued Operations: $(58,839,000) for the nine months ended September 30, 2025, primarily due to the termination of the CK Distribution acquisition.
  • Net Income (Loss): $(72,900,000) for the nine months ended September 30, 2025, compared to $(29,670,000) for the same period in 2024, reflecting the impact of discontinued operations.
  • Basic Net Income (Loss) Per Common Share: $(0.00) for the nine months ended September 30, 2025, consistent with the previous year.
  • Diluted Net Income (Loss) Per Common Share: $(0.00) for the nine months ended September 30, 2025, indicating no change from the prior year.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: Fast Casual Concepts, Inc. recognized revenue from digital marketing services totaling $44,600,000 for the nine months ended September 30, 2025. This marks a significant shift from previous operations focused on franchising and specialty drink mixes.
  • Discontinued Operations: The company terminated its acquisition of CK Distribution, LLC, which was involved in the production and sale of specialty drink mixes. This decision was part of a strategic shift away from beverage distribution to focus on digital marketing services.
  • Operational Shift: Fast Casual has fully transitioned from its previous business model of developing and franchising casual eating establishments to focusing on digital marketing services through its subsidiary, GDS Lumina, Inc.
  • Future Outlook: The company plans to continue its focus on digital marketing services and is seeking to raise additional capital to support this new direction. Management anticipates that losses from operations will increase in the short term due to necessary staffing and increased legal and accounting expenses.
  • Going Concern: There is substantial doubt about the company's ability to continue as a going concern due to accumulated losses and negative cash flows from operations. Fast Casual is actively seeking additional equity or debt financing to support its operations and achieve profitability.

SEC Filing: FAST CASUAL CONCEPTS, INC. [ FCCI ] - 10-Q - Nov. 14, 2025

data.title: Catalyst Crew Technologies Corp. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Catalyst Crew Technologies Corp., a company transitioning from big data analytics in the gaming and gambling industry to facial recognition technology solutions, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter of 2025. The report provides insights into the company's financial performance and significant business changes during this period.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $0, The company earned no revenues for the three and nine months ended September 30, 2025, and 2024.
  • Operating Expenses: $27,935 for the three months ended September 30, 2025, compared to $33,668 for the same period in 2024. The decrease was due to reduced professional fees.
  • Operating Expenses: $63,756 for the nine months ended September 30, 2025, compared to $2,315,892 for the same period in 2024. The significant decrease was due to reduced stock-based compensation.
  • Net Loss: $36,302 for the three months ended September 30, 2025, compared to $43,481 for the same period in 2024. The decrease in net loss was associated with reduced operating expenses.
  • Net Loss: $89,939 for the nine months ended September 30, 2025, compared to $2,343,608 for the same period in 2024. The decrease in net loss was associated with reduced operating expenses.
  • Net Loss Per Common Share - Basic and Diluted: $(0.00) for the three months ended September 30, 2025, compared to $(0.17) for the same period in 2024.
  • Net Loss Per Common Share - Basic and Diluted: $(0.00) for the nine months ended September 30, 2025, compared to $(9.14) for the same period in 2024.

Business Highlights

  • Business Model Transition: Catalyst Crew Technologies Corp. has undergone significant changes in its business model, shifting from big data analytics in the gaming and gambling industry to focusing on facial recognition technology solutions. This transition was marked by the acquisition of facial recognition technology assets from Mr. Waqas Nakhwa.
  • Leadership Changes: The company experienced several leadership changes, with Mr. Waqas Nakhwa taking over as President, CEO, CFO, Treasurer, Secretary, and Chairman of the Board following the resignation of Ms. Gurneet Kaur. These changes are part of the company's strategic realignment.
  • Operational Focus: The company has ceased its previous operations related to big data analytics and is now concentrating on developing and deploying facial recognition technology solutions. This shift is intended to align with the company's new strategic direction.
  • Future Outlook: Management anticipates an increase in operating expenses in future years due to the costs associated with increased operational activity under the new business model. The company is focused on securing funding to support its operational and expansion plans.
  • Going Concern: The company has expressed substantial doubt about its ability to continue as a going concern due to ongoing net losses and accumulated deficits. The company is actively seeking funding through securities sales or strategic partnerships to sustain operations.

SEC Filing: Catalyst Crew Technologies Corp. [ CCTC ] - 10-Q - Nov. 14, 2025

data.title: CalEthos, Inc. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
CalEthos, Inc., a company focused on developing large-scale data center campuses, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter of 2025. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance and significant business developments over the past nine months.

Financial Highlights

Revenues: $0. The company reported no revenues for the nine months ended September 30, 2025, and 2024.

Net Loss: $(5.74) million. The net loss for the nine months ended September 30, 2025, decreased from $(9.33) million in the same period in 2024, primarily due to a decrease in financing costs and abandoned project costs.

Net Loss Per Share - Basic and Diluted: $(0.22). The net loss per share improved from $(0.37) in the nine months ended September 30, 2024.

Total Operating Expenses: $0.71 million. Operating expenses decreased by 14.5% compared to the same period in 2024, driven by reductions in professional fees and equity-based compensation.

Total Other Expenses: $(5.03) million. A significant reduction from $(8.49) million in the prior year, largely due to lower financing costs and abandoned project costs.

Business Highlights

Business Restructuring: In July 2022, CalEthos, Inc. restructured its business to focus exclusively on developing a large-scale data center campus, initially in Imperial County, California. This strategic shift included considering acquisitions or joint ventures in the clean energy or data center infrastructure industries.

Project Abandonment: By May 2025, the company decided not to renew its purchase option on a 315-acre parcel in Imperial County due to delays in regulatory approvals, including additional environmental studies and unresolved community concerns. This led to a shift in development efforts to other locations with more favorable regulatory environments.

Formation of TerraVolt Infrastructure Inc.: In May 2025, CalEthos formed TerraVolt, a wholly-owned subsidiary aimed at providing sustainable, baseload, powered land and infrastructure solutions for large-scale data centers. TerraVolt's Infrastructure-as-a-Service (IaaS) Platform will integrate grid and behind-the-meter power with construction-ready data center sites.

Operational Focus: The company is now focusing on properties in states that allow onsite power production using natural gas fuel cells and turbines, with access to natural gas pipelines. The company is also considering geothermal power but only as a co-developer and user of the power produced.

Team Expansion: TerraVolt has assembled a team of land use and data center experts to evaluate locations with favorable energy resources and supportive local, county, and state officials for timely power plant construction and data center development.

Future Outlook: CalEthos plans to raise funds through issuing common stock, preferred stock, and/or debt securities to support its business plan. The company is in discussions with potential funding sources to secure the necessary capital for its operations and development plans.

SEC Filing: CalEthos, Inc. [ GEDC ] - 10-Q - Nov. 14, 2025

data.title: Moody's Upgrades Its Outlook on Banks But Flags Deregulation as Risk — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Rebecca Ungarino

Moody's Ratings now has a rosier outlook on the U.S. banking system over the next 12 to 18 months because the firm expects a mix of lower interest rates and "muted but stable" economic growth to help lenders' profitability and asset quality.

The ratings firm lifted its outlook for the economically sensitive sector to stable from negative while noting it expects real gross domestic product growth in the U.S. to slow to 1.8% in 2026 from 2% this year.

"The stable outlook also incorporates an expectation that a modest pickup in loan growth and a steepening yield curve will enhance net interest income, most banks' primary revenue source," Moody's said Thursday.

For Moody's, a shift in outlook is different from a change in rating, which remains the same, at Aa1 stable. The latest quarter of earnings from large banks, including JPMorgan Chase, Wells Fargo and Bank of America, showed solid or improving consumer and commercial credit, with generally upbeat comments from executives on the economy.

At the same time, the Moody's said that expectations of a lighter touch from financial regulators adds some risk to the banking sector.

"The prospect of a loosening of bank regulation and supervision adds some incremental risk for bank creditors," Moody's wrote. "Greater clarity on bank capital rules and stress testing will likely lead to lower capital ratios at the largest banks."

This is a developing story. Please check back for updates.

Write to Rebecca Ungarino at rebecca.ungarino@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

TIGER GLOBAL MANAGEMENT LLC:

data.description:
Plains All American Pipeline L.P. (PAA) filed a Form 8K - Other Events - with the U.S Securities and Exchange Commission on November 14, 2025.

On November 10, 2025, the Issuers entered into an underwriting agreement (the "Underwriting Agreement") with Citigroup Global Markets Inc., CIBC World Markets Corp., RBC Capital Markets, LLC, and SMBC Nikko Securities America, Inc., as representatives of the several underwriters named therein (collectively, the "Underwriters"), relating to the Offering.

The Notes were offered and sold under the Issuers' shelf registration statement on Form S-3 (Registration No. 333-281967) filed with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission on September 6, 2024 (the "Registration Statement"), and are described in the prospectus supplement.

The Underwriting Agreement is filed as Exhibit 1.1 to this Current Report on Form 8-K and is incorporated herein by reference.

The full text of this SEC filing can be retrieved at: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1070423/000110465925112281/tm2531286d1_8k.htm

data.title: Plains All American Files 8K - Entry Into Definitive Agreement >PAA
data.description:
Plains All American Pipeline L.P. (PAA) filed a Form 8K - Entry Into a Definitive Agreement - with the U.S Securities and Exchange Commission on November 14, 2025.

On November 14, 2025, Plains All American Pipeline, L.P. ("PAA") and PAA Finance Corp. (together with PAA, the "Issuers") completed the public offering (the "Offering") of $750 million aggregate principal amount of the Issuers' debt securities, consisting of $300 million aggregate principal amount of 4.700% Senior Notes due 2031 (the "2031 Notes") and $450 million aggregate principal amount of 5.600% Senior Notes due 2036 (the "2036 Notes," and, together with the 2031 Notes, the "Notes"). The 2031 Notes and the 2036 Notes were offered as additional issuances of the Issuers' 4.700% Senior Notes due 2031 and 5.600% Senior Notes due 2036, of which $700 million and $550 million aggregate principal amount, respectively, were issued on September 8, 2025. The 2031 Notes and 2036 Notes form a single series with the initial notes of their respective series, have identical terms as the initial notes of their respective series, and have the same CUSIP number as and trade interchangeably with the initial notes of their respective series. Immediately following the issuance of the 2031 Notes issued in the Offering, there are $1 billion aggregate principal amount of the 2031 Notes outstanding. Immediately following the issuance of the 2036 Notes issued in the Offering, there are $1 billion aggregate principal amount of the 2036 Notes outstanding.

The terms of the Notes are governed by the indenture (the "Base Indenture," and as amended and supplemented by the Supplemental Indentures (defined below), the "Indenture") dated as of September 25, 2002 by and among the Issuers and U.S. Bank Trust Company, National Association (as successor trustee), as trustee (the "Trustee"), as supplemented by the Thirty-Fifth Supplemental Indenture dated as of September 8, 2025 by and among the Issuers and the Trustee (the "35th Supplemental Indenture") relating to the issuance of the 2031 Notes and the Thirty-Sixth Supplemental Indenture dated as of September 8, 2025 by and among the Issuers and the Trustee (the "36th Supplemental Indenture," and, together with the 35th Supplemental Indenture, the "Supplemental Indentures") relating to the issuance of the 2036 Notes.

The 2031 Notes will mature on January 15, 2031, and the 2036 Notes will mature on January 15, 2036. Interest is payable on the Notes on each January 15 and July 15, commencing on January 15, 2026. The Issuers may redeem some or all of the Notes at any time and from time to time prior to maturity at the redemption prices specified in the Indenture.

The Notes are PAA's senior unsecured obligations, will rank equally in right of payment with all of PAA's existing and future senior debt, and will rank senior in right of payment to all of PAA's future subordinated debt. The Notes will be effectively subordinated to all of PAA's existing and future secured debt to the extent of the value of the collateral securing such indebtedness.

In certain circumstances, the Indenture restricts PAA's ability and the ability of certain of its subsidiaries to: (i) enter into sale and leaseback transactions; (ii) incur liens; (iii) merge or consolidate with another company; and (iv) transfer and sell assets. These covenants are subject to a number of important exceptions and qualifications.

The Indenture contains customary events of default with respect to the Notes of either series, including:

· default in any payment of interest on any Note of that series when due, continued for 60 days;

· default in the payment of principal of or premium, if any, on any Note of that series when due;

· failure by PAA to comply with its obligations under the Indenture, in certain cases subject to notice and grace periods;

· payment defaults and accelerations with respect to other indebtedness of PAA and its Subsidiaries (as defined in the Indenture) in the aggregate principal amount of $150.0 million or more;

· certain events of bankruptcy, insolvency or reorganization of PAA or, if and so long as the Notes of that series are guaranteed by a subsidiary guarantor, by such subsidiary guarantor; or

· if and so long as the Notes of that series are guaranteed by a subsidiary guarantor:

· the guarantee by such subsidiary guarantor ceases to be in full force and effect, except as otherwise provided in the Indenture;

· the guarantee by such subsidiary guarantor is declared null and void in a judicial proceeding; or

· such subsidiary guarantor denies or disaffirms its obligations under the Indenture or its guarantee.

If an event of default under the Indenture occurs and is continuing, the Trustee or the holders of at least 25% in principal amount of the outstanding Notes may declare the principal of, premium, if any, and accrued and unpaid interest, if any, on the Notes to be due and payable, or, in the case of certain events of default relating to bankruptcy, insolvency or reorganization, those amounts will automatically become immediately due and payable.

Other material terms of the Notes, the Base Indenture and the Supplemental Indentures are described in the prospectus supplement, dated November 10, 2025 (the "prospectus supplement"), to the prospectus, dated September 6, 2024, which is included in the Registration Statement (as defined below). The foregoing descriptions of the Indenture and the Notes are qualified in their entirety by reference to such Indenture (including the forms of Notes attached thereto). Copies of the 35th Supplemental Indenture and the 36th Supplemental Indenture are filed as Exhibit 4.1 and Exhibit 4.3, respectively, to this Current Report on Form 8-K and are incorporated herein by reference.

The full text of this SEC filing can be retrieved at: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1070423/000110465925112281/tm2531286d1_8k.htm

Any exhibits and associated documents for this SEC filing can be retrieved at: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1070423/000110465925112281/0001104659-25-112281-index.htm

Public companies must file a Form 8-K, or current report, with the SEC generally within four days of any event that could materially affect a company's financial position or the value of its shares.
